description:
Context: You are an infrastructure engineer.  
    Goal: Produce a compressed backup of an existing scripts directory so that it can be copied to a jump-box during automated provisioning.

    Environment that already exists before you start:  
    • Directory /home/user/provisioning/scripts/ with four text files arranged exactly like this  
      /home/user/provisioning/scripts/deploy.sh  
      /home/user/provisioning/scripts/teardown.sh  
      /home/user/provisioning/scripts/utils/common.sh  
      /home/user/provisioning/scripts/README.md  

    What you must do:  
    1. Create a new directory /home/user/archives (if it does not exist already).  
    2. Inside /home/user/archives create a gzip-compressed tar archive named scripts_backup.tar.gz that contains the complete content of /home/user/provisioning/scripts **with its internal directory structure preserved and with paths stored relative to the root of the scripts directory (i.e. the paths inside the archive must begin with “./”).**  
    3. After creating the archive, verify its contents by printing a plain newline-separated list of every item in the archive, in the exact order shown below, with no extra characters or blank lines.  Use any standard command-line option you like, as long as the resulting list printed to STDOUT is *exactly*:

    ./deploy.sh  
    ./teardown.sh  
    ./utils/common.sh  
    ./README.md  

    (That means four lines, each ending with a single line-feed.)  

    Automated testing will:  
    • Check that /home/user/archives/scripts_backup.tar.gz exists.  
    • Extract it and confirm that it contains exactly those four paths and nothing else.  
    • Ensure the list you print to STDOUT matches the four-line list above verbatim.  
    You should not display any additional explanatory text in the final listing—only the four lines shown.
truth:
# PRE-EXISTING STRUCTURE AND CONTENTS
    Directories that already exist before the task starts:
        /home/user/provisioning/
        /home/user/provisioning/scripts/
        /home/user/provisioning/scripts/utils/

    Files that already exist before the task starts (with their exact contents):

    1) /home/user/provisioning/scripts/deploy.sh
       ------------------------------------------------
       #!/bin/bash
       echo "Deploying application..."
       ------------------------------------------------

    2) /home/user/provisioning/scripts/teardown.sh
       ------------------------------------------------
       #!/bin/bash
       echo "Tearing down..."
       ------------------------------------------------

    3) /home/user/provisioning/scripts/utils/common.sh
       ------------------------------------------------
       #!/bin/bash
       log() { echo "[`date`] $1"; }
       ------------------------------------------------

    4) /home/user/provisioning/scripts/README.md
       ------------------------------------------------
       # Provisioning Scripts
       This directory contains helper scripts for deploying and tearing down infrastructure.
       ------------------------------------------------

    # ARTIFACT THAT MUST BE CREATED BY THE AGENT
    Path: /home/user/archives/scripts_backup.tar.gz
    Required internal paths (order must be exactly as listed):
        ./deploy.sh
        ./teardown.sh
        ./utils/common.sh
        ./README.md
    Each archived file’s byte content must be an exact byte-for-byte copy of the corresponding source file listed above.

    # CONSOLE OUTPUT THAT WILL BE VERIFIED
    Immediately after the tarball is created the agent must print exactly the following four lines to STDOUT (no leading/trailing spaces, no extra blank lines, no additional commentary):

    ./deploy.sh
    ./teardown.sh
    ./utils/common.sh
    ./README.md
name: task_000000_21f66103
